description: Ketubba (marriage contract) fragment describing that one-third of a house is given to a daughter upon marriage, on the condition that it would become her property only after her mother’s death; the boundaries of the house are precisely delimited. The document begins with the clause detailing the sums of the marriage payment, and the trousseau list contains three types of skullcaps, a bed cover and four cushions, a pair of earrings and a gold pin, two garments with borders of another color, and a shawl, amongst other items. (Friedman, Jewish Marriage, vol. 2, 332-8) EMS
description_he: שבר כתובה: שליש בית ניתן לבת לרגל נישואיה, אולם יהיה קנינה רק לאחר מות אמה. הנדוניה כוללת כיפות, כיסוי מיטה, כריות, עגילים, סיכת זהב ובגדים.
